data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <bead id="ddx-ff7c8ec9">
    <title>runs: specs (FEAT-008/010/019) + cross-cutting e2e + audit event</title>
    <description>
Update FEAT-008 (web UI), FEAT-010 (run architecture), FEAT-019 (agent eval) for the run-detail surface. Cross-cutting e2e covering view → drill → download. Audit event for run-detail access (project membership = visibility).
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. All three FEATs updated. 2. Cross-cutting e2e passes. 3. Audit event recorded on view.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, story:16, area:specs, kind:doc</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-a8a18cb7" base-rev="5f8415793765d59ca580a77fa7c08c30f9b714aa" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260505T022328-66a80ffa"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260505T022328-66a80ffa/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260505T022328-66a80ffa/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260505T022328-66a80ffa/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260505T022328-66a80ffa/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260505T022328-66a80ffa/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Implemented and committed as `eb360e3` (`fix: audit canonical run detail views [ddx-ff7c8ec9]`).

What changed:
- Added project-scoped `run` detail auditing in [resolver_runs.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-ff7c8ec9-20260505T022328-66a80ffa/cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_runs.go#L63), including:
  - visibility check against the current project
  - one `run_detail_view` event per distinct run id
  - audit body fields `project_id`, `run_id`, `layer`, and `visibility=project_membership`
- Added coverage in [resolver_runs_test.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-ff7c8ec9-20260505T022328-66a80ffa/cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_runs_test.go#L111) asserting:
  - run detail loads succeed for the scoped project
  - `run_detail_view` lands on the originating bead
  - the event is emitted once per distinct run detail
- Synced the three feature specs:
  - [FEAT-008](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-ff7c8ec9-20260505T022328-66a80ffa/docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-web-ui.md#L769)
  - [FEAT-010](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-ff7c8ec9-20260505T022328-66a80ffa/docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-executions.md#L535)
  - [FEAT-019](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-ff7c8ec9-20260505T022328-66a80ffa/docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-019-agent-evaluation.md#L61)

Verification:
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/graphql/... ./internal/server/...`
- `lefthook run pre-commit` passed during commit
- The hook’s `go-test` job needed `CGO_ENABLED=1` because it runs with `-race`

Working tree is clean.
